headline_subtitle: Cancer, NAD, fat — and why most of what you've read is half the story.

cards.lead: Why Blood NAD Tests Miss What's Happening Inside the Cell:
cards.body: {{concept:Charles Brenner}}'s lab published data showing that people with mitochondrial disease have low blood {{concept:NAD+}} — and used it as evidence for the standard 'top up NAD with supplements' framing. Kruse pushes back: blood NAD is the wrong compartment to measure. The biophysical claim is that NAD concentration is a downstream readout of cellular field state — fix the field, the NAD follows. Topping up the blood doesn't move the cellular number.

Charles Brenner.text: Biochemist (UC Irvine / City of Hope), developer of NAD quantitation technology. Principal voice for the canonical NAD-precursor-supplementation hypothesis in aging and mitochondrial disease.

NAD+.text: Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide — central electron-carrier coenzyme in mitochondrial energy metabolism. Cellular NAD+ levels gate oxidative phosphorylation throughput; the supplement industry assumes raising blood NAD+ raises intracellular NAD+, a leap Kruse disputes.

cards.source_quote: my lab developed NAD quantitation tech and showed, with @ASW_lab, that people with mito disease have low blood NAD…

cards.lead: Heavy Water Turns Calorie-Burning Brown Fat Into Storage Fat:
cards.body: A research thread documented that {{concept:BAT}} (the kind of fat that burns calories as heat) is highest in infants and declines through adulthood — the standard reading is just 'aging metabolism.' Kruse adds a mechanism: rising body {{concept:deuterium}} load is what destroys BAT mitochondria and converts the cells into {{concept:WAT}} (storage fat). The downstream consequence: more {{concept:NAFLD}}. One coherent mechanistic chain for two parallel modern trends.

BAT.text: Brown Adipose Tissue — fat tissue rich in mitochondria that burns calories directly as heat (thermogenesis). Highest in infants; declines through adulthood.

WAT.text: White Adipose Tissue — the storage form of fat that accumulates with positive energy balance. Mitochondrially poor compared to BAT.

deuterium.text: Heavy isotope of hydrogen (one extra neutron). Behaves like regular hydrogen chemically but is twice as massive — when it ends up inside mitochondrial machinery it jams the rotation of ATP synthase. Kruse's central environmental-toxin claim.

NAFLD.text: Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease — abnormal lipid accumulation in liver cells absent significant alcohol intake. Now the most common chronic liver condition in industrialised populations.

cards.source_quote: Brown adipose tissue activity peaks in infancy and declines progressively through adulthood. By the fourth decade, thermogenic capacity is largely lost.

cards.lead: Cancer Cells CAN Burn Fat — The Ketogenic Story Is Incomplete:
cards.body: Common claim circulating in cancer-nutrition discourse, attributed to the {{concept:Seyfried}}-lineage: 'cancer cells can't efficiently oxidize fatty acids because of defective mitochondria, so >80% of their ATP comes from fermentation.' Kruse calls this flatly wrong — tumor metabolism is plastic enough to run on fats once glucose is cut. The implication: 'starve the tumor with keto' is an oversimplification that can dangerously delay real treatment in advanced patients.

Seyfried.text: Thomas Seyfried — Boston College biologist, lead proponent of the 'cancer as a metabolic disease' framework. Best known for the ketogenic-diet-starves-tumor thesis built on Otto Warburg's glucose-dependence observation.

cards.source_quote: Cancer cells can't oxidize fatty acids efficiently due to their defective mitochondria. More than 80% of their ATPs come from fermentation…

cards.lead: Why Cancer Cells Prefer Sugar — A Different Explanation:
cards.body: The famous {{concept:Warburg shift}} (cancer cells fermenting glucose even with oxygen available) is usually read as a mitochondrial defect. Kruse's reframe: the cell is actively trying to {{concept:de-fragging}} its own internal water lattice of accumulated deuterium. The sugar-burning is the cleanup tool, not the disease. Same observation, opposite causal story.

Warburg shift.text: Otto Warburg's 1924 observation: cancer cells preferentially use aerobic glycolysis (fermenting glucose to lactate even with oxygen present) instead of full oxidative phosphorylation. Mainstream reading: mitochondrial defect. Kruse: deliberate metabolic reroute to manage internal deuterium.

de-fragging.text: Kruse-coined term for restoring the structured (EZ) water lattice coherence inside the cell, reversing the disordered state that sets in under deuterium + nnEMF load. Analogous to defragmenting a fragmented disk, but for the intracellular water phase.

cards.source_quote: WHY THE WARBURG SHIFT HAPPENS IN CANCER? The cell is trying to de-frag its own cytoplasm of deuterium ya' big Dummy.

cards.lead: Kentucky's Hidden Limestone Caves + Atlantic Currents:
cards.body: Surfacing the Mississippian limestone {{concept:karst}} aquifer system under Kentucky as a regional biophysical case study — one of the most extensive interconnected groundwater networks on Earth. Paired with a one-line {{concept:AMOC}} flag. Reads as another node in the dipole-decline story: aquifer dielectric properties are coupled to ocean-current state.

karst.text: Landscape formed by the dissolution of soluble bedrock (limestone, dolomite, gypsum) — produces caves, sinkholes, and large interconnected groundwater systems. Kentucky sits on one of Earth's most extensive karst aquifers.

AMOC.text: Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation — the system of ocean currents that moves warm surface water poleward and cold deep water back south. Its weakening is a flagship indicator of climate-system reorganisation.

cards.source_quote: Many do not know Kentucky sits on top of one of the most expansive, interconnected Mississippian limestone karst systems on Earth.

bullets.title: What NAD+ Actually Is — A Biophysics Reframe
bullets.summary: New thread reframing {{concept:NAD+}} away from the supplementation/aging narrative and toward a structural-biophysics reading: NAD+ as a state variable of mitochondrial coupling, not a consumable molecule to top up via pills. Direct extension of today's blood-NAD tweet — same critique, longer form.

NAD+.text: Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide — central electron-carrier coenzyme in mitochondrial energy metabolism. Kruse's reframe treats its concentration as a downstream readout of cellular field state, not an independent lever.

bullets.title: How Spiral Vortices Affect Living Tissue
bullets.summary: New thread on the geometric-vortex framework — 7/12/21-turn copper spiral resonators, water-lattice coherence, and the Chromosome 2 interstitial telomeric loop coupling. Continuation of the 'geometry alters material physics' line; pairs with today's broader biophysics-vs-chemistry frame.

bullets.title: Connective Tissue, Joint Pain, and Heavy Water
bullets.summary: New thread linking fascia and joint integrity to systemic {{concept:deuterium}} load. Reads as the structural-tissue counterpart to today's deuterium → brown-fat → liver chain — same mechanism (mitochondria poisoned by heavy water), different tissue (connective vs. adipose).

deuterium.text: Heavy isotope of hydrogen (one extra neutron). Chemically like hydrogen but twice as massive. When it ends up inside mitochondrial ATP-synthase machinery it jams the rotor. Kruse's central environmental-toxin claim.
